title: 4 marks of a man who governs himself
caption:
Most men can quote the chapter. They just can't make themselves live it.

That's not a faith problem. That's a governance problem.

Proverbs 12:1 says a man who loves discipline loves knowledge. But discipline isn't a personality type — it's a set of decisions made before the moment arrives. Before the phone is in your hand. Before she texts back. Before the third one.

Here are 4 marks of a man who governs himself:

→ He governs his tongue. The first thing that betrays a man is what comes out of his mouth. He notices the 3 seconds before he responds.

→ He controls what he lets in. Your phone feed is a curriculum. What you watch, scroll, and return to shapes your default. The private tab doesn't stay private.

→ He decides before the moment. A man who decides when he's already tempted has already lost. He names his limits in the daylight, not when he's being tested.

→ He submits the battle. Self-control is a fruit — Galatians 5:23. You pursue the Spirit. The Spirit builds the governance.

You don't get governed overnight. But you start by deciding you're going to be that man.

—

Comment "marks" and I'll send you the link to ETHOS.

#ethos #christianmen #faithoverfear #godlymen #manofgod #disciplineequalsfreedom #masculinity #selfcontrol #christianity #scripture
theme: He runs his life — his impulses don't control him.
